svg.text: The system shall provide the following functional capabilities:
The system shall authenticate users based on role-specific ID format and PIN/password.
The system shall create server-side sessions upon successful login and destroy them on logout.
Students shall be able to submit anonymous feedback with drag-based half-star ratings (0.5 to 5.0).
Each feedback submission shall generate a unique cryptographic Reference ID (FB-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX).
Students shall be able to verify their submission using the Reference ID without revealing identity.
The system shall validate that the submitted course belongs to the student's registered department.
HODs shall access a real-time analytics dashboard for their department with per-course ratings.
HODs shall be able to download a formatted PDF report of department analytics with one click.
HODs shall be able to add, edit, and remove custom questions from the department feedback form.
Admins shall be able to create, activate, and deactivate time-bound feedback windows.
Admins shall manage departments, courses, and faculty assignments institution-wide.
Faculty shall be able to view anonymized feedback analytics for their assigned courses.
All API endpoints shall implement rate limiting. Auth: 10/15min. Feedback: 20/10min. Global: 100/15min.
Student login identity shall never be persisted in or linked to any feedback record in the database.
The HOD dashboard shall load within 3 seconds on a standard broadband connection.
The interface shall be fully responsive and usable on screen widths from 320px to 4K resolution.
The system shall handle concurrent submissions without data loss or integrity violations.
All API inputs shall be validated using Zod schemas to prevent runtime type errors.
The application shall be deployable as a PWA, installable on Android, iOS, and desktop browsers.
The PostgreSQL schema shall support addition of new departments without structural migration.
The technologies selected — React 18, Node.js, Express.js, and PostgreSQL — are all mature, open-source,
and widely supported. The development environment (cloud platform with pnpm workspaces) provides
immediate deployment capability. The team possesses the necessary skills in JavaScript/TypeScript
development. All tools are freely available with extensive documentation.
The entire system is built using open-source technologies with no licensing cost. The hosting platform
provides free-tier infrastructure for development and basic production use. The only recurring cost is
database hosting, which is included in the platform subscription. The development effort of one
developer over one academic semester is the primary cost, estimated in Chapter 7.
The system replaces a familiar paper-based process with a digital equivalent that is simpler to use.
Students submit feedback via any web browser — no app installation required. HODs access analytics
without any special training. Admins manage the system through an intuitive panel. A PWA interface
further reduces the learning curve by providing a native-app-like experience.
The project was planned to be completed within one academic semester (approximately 5 months). The scope
was defined conservatively to ensure all core modules — authentication, feedback submission, HOD
analytics, admin management, and PDF generation — could be fully implemented and tested within the
CUPGS Academic Feedback Manager | Software Engineering Project Report
